page: Microsoft Azure D48als_v7 is a virtual machine instance from the Dalsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports the x86_64 architecture. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute capabilities. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance, passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth write operations fall into the strong category (top 25%). The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions, which contribute to top-tier performance in small-model LLM inference prompt processing and text generation.

The primary tradeoff of the D48als_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive workloads. Additionally, the lack of bundled local storage requires external storage provisioning. However, from a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high clock speed of 4.5 GHz and dedicated CPU allocation provide high compute density per resource unit. This makes it a cost-effective option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints.

This instance is well-suited for compute-heavy workloads such as high-throughput web serving, batch processing, CPU-based machine learning inference on smaller models, and compression or decompression tasks. It is also appropriate for general-purpose applications with moderate memory requirements that benefit from high single-core and multi-core CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure D48als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 96.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks bundled local storage but is premium storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, aided by AVX-512 instruction support. This resource profile offers high compute density, making it qualitatively cost-effective for CPU-bound tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as high-throughput web serving, data compression, and small-model machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 96 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48als_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for CPU-bound tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a low memory-to-core ratio for intensive CPU workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs.
Configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and data compression performance.
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector operations.
No bundled local storage, but compatible with premium external storage.
Optimized for high-throughput web serving and CPU-bound batch processing.
